---
title: "LRP191: GP knee-test — intervention sessions (IS) -> word reading (W)"

format:
  html:
    code-fold: true
    theme: cosmo
    number-sections: true
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    code-links:
      - repo
---

{{< include _partials/_header.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_setup.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_gate_badge.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_key_findings.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_reading_guide.qmd >}}

## Overview

LRP191 is a **new** dose-response **GP knee-test**: does the amount of intervention delivered show
a **"knee"** — a number of sessions beyond which more sessions go with a more marked difference in
word reading — the way [LRP58](../lrp-rli-mech-058) found for letter sounds?

The `dose_response` family (LRP77 and companions) fits sessions as a straight-line / period-varying
slope and has no curve in which to find a knee; this model instead fits the session exposure as a
nonparametric **HSGP** curve, so a knee can be looked for. It is the first continuous-covariate GP
mechanism in the suite: the readiness knee is reported in the exposure's own **raw units** (sessions
attended in a period), not a bounded count. `target_accept` is **0.999** (per LRP58).

_Exposure — per-period sessions, not cumulative dose._ The exposure is per-period sessions attended (`attend`), the same exposure LRP77 uses — **not**
cumulative dose. Cumulative attendance is a running sum of the $IS$ collider and, if *conditioned
on*, reopens the latent-$GA$ backdoor (see LRP77 / #269); here sessions is the exposure, not an
adjuster. `attend` has 55 missing values (the pre-/no-intervention rows, where a per-period session
count is undefined); these survive as NaN and are dropped by the factory's exposure keep-mask, so
the fit uses exactly the on-intervention rows that actually carry a dose.

## Adjustment set

Revised DAG (2026-07-10), re-derived by a backdoor d-separation search with the latent general
ability ($GA$) held (the criterion that reproduces LRP58 and dose-077 exactly): $IS$'s parents are
$\{A, GA, IG\}$ and nothing else, so the minimal observed set blocking every backdoor to word
reading is $\{A, IG\}$. Group $G\,(= IG)$ is the always-in term, $A$ the linear age precision
covariate, and $W_{\text{pre}}$ the autoregressive baseline. No measure or hearing/speech adjusters
are needed (they are not parents of $IS$). This is **identical adjustment to dose-077** — only the
functional form (HSGP curve vs period-varying slope) changes.

_Strictly observational — never a treatment effect._ Session dose was **not** randomised (how much a child attended reflects ability, attendance and
availability) and $IS$ is a partial collider, so the curve is an **adjusted association /
sensitivity view**, never a treatment effect. Residual confounding by latent general ability ($GA$)
remains. Read it as "children who attended more scored higher, and here is whether that
relationship bends" — **not** "more sessions cause faster progress". The randomised causal claim
lives in the ITT suite.

![Model diagram](model_graph.png){#fig-model .lightbox fig-align="left"}

{{< include _partials/_priors.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_prior_predictive.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_results_mechanism.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_technical.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_footer.qmd >}}
